description: A detective has intelligence that specific digital files linked to a financial fraud ring have been distributed across suspects' devices. The forensic lab has compiled MD5 hashes of these known contraband files into a hash set. Create a forensic case named 'Hash_Lookup_2024' (Case Number: 'INV-HASH-006') and import the hash set from '/home/ga/evidence/known_bad_hashset.txt' as a 'Notable' set named 'FraudRing_Hashes'. Analyze the data source '/home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd' using the Hash Lookup and File Type Identification modules to identify matching files. Document the results in a pipe-delimited report at '/home/ga/Reports/hash_hits_report.txt' using the format: MD5_HASH|FILENAME|FILE_PATH|FILE_SIZE_BYTES|ALLOCATED_STATUS (where status is 'ALLOCATED' or 'DELETED'). Additionally, save a summary to '/home/ga/Reports/hash_hits_summary.txt' containing the hash set name, total hashes in set, counts of hashes with and without matches, total matching files, and a list of hits in the format: HIT: <md5> -> <filename>.
